title: Infographic report
description: Data contract for the infographic format: a full-bleed vertical visual-narrative poster. The page is a vertical stack of 100vw coloured bands (alternating white / --navy-bg tint / --bg-band soft / one --bg-deep dark). Each band is one poster stage identified by a kicker number and kind. Oversized tnum numbers lead every stage; CSS/SVG-drawn diagram primitives (hero stat bursts, process flow, funnel, pyramid, cycle, comparison block) carry the story. Captions replace paragraphs. Default theme: Green. The sample's full HTML is the canonical rendered instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

reportType.description: Eyebrow category label shown in the hero band, e.g. 'State of Digital Onboarding'.

title.description: Poster main title shown oversized in the hero band, e.g. 'The State of Digital Onboarding 2026'.

subtitle.description: Optional standfirst sentence (2–3 lines) that appears directly under the title in the hero band.

date.description: ISO date the infographic was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

chips.description: Optional mono pills shown in the hero band under the standfirst, e.g. survey scope or methodology chips.

items.description: Chip label text, e.g. 'SURVEY N = 4,206,118', 'FY2025 · 18 MARKETS'.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. Infographic default is 'green'.

bands.description: Ordered sequence of poster bands. The first band should be kind='hero'. Subsequent bands each carry one diagram kind. The last content band is typically kind='takeaway' before the source footer.

kind.description: The poster diagram / layout type for this band. 'hero' = opening stat-burst row with title and standfirst. 'stat' = one or more large tnum numbers (stat-burst grid). 'process' = horizontal numbered process-flow steps (proc-flow). 'funnel' = tapering funnel stages with values and drop-off labels. 'pyramid' = layered value/maturity pyramid (pyr-tier stack). 'cycle' = continuous lifecycle/improvement cycle ring with nodes. 'compare' = two-column then/now or A-vs-B comparison on a dark band. 'takeaway' = closing single stat-burst + lede sentence.

bg.description: Band background variant. 'white' = plain white (default); 'tint' = --navy-bg brand tint (.band--tint); 'soft' = --bg-band soft grey (.band--soft); 'deep' = --bg-deep dark inverted (.band--deep, light ink).

kicker.description: Optional mono kicker label for this stage, e.g. '01 / THE JOURNEY'. The number prefix is rendered in the brand accent colour. Omit for the hero band.

heading.description: Optional stage heading shown at 24–34px/800 (band-head) below the kicker.

subtext.description: Optional 1–2 sentence caption (band-sub) beneath the heading. This is the maximum prose for any band; one line is ideal.

items.description: Band content items. Shape varies by kind. hero/stat: each item is a stat-burst ({bignum, unit, label, note, delta, deltaDir, state}). process: each item is a step ({n, title, desc, stat}). funnel: each item is a stage ({label, sublabel, value, widthPct, dropLabel?}). pyramid: each item is a tier ({label, sublabel, value}). cycle: each item is a node ({label, desc}). compare: two items (each {tag, bignum, unit, caption, rows:[{label,value}], state?}). takeaway: single stat-burst item.
Band item. Required fields vary by band kind — see 'items' description above.

stats.description: Alias for 'items' used specifically for stat-burst collections (hero burst-row and stat bands). Kept as an alternative key for clarity in hero bands.

bignum.description: Oversized tnum number shown in the burst (string to preserve formatting), e.g. '68', '4', '$1.4'.

unit.description: Unit shown in a smaller superscript after the bignum, e.g. '%', 'm 51s', 'B'.

label.description: Tracked mono label under the bignum, e.g. 'End-to-end completion'.

note.description: Optional one-sentence burst note under the label.

delta.description: Optional delta label, e.g. '14 pts YoY', '−47% faster'.

deltaDir.description: Direction of the delta arrow: 'up' (ok colour), 'down' (risk colour).

state.description: Optional semantic colour for the bignum itself: 'ok' = brand ok green, 'hold' = amber, 'risk' = bordeaux, omit for the default brand navy.

source.description: Source / methodology footnote block rendered in the poster footer band.

notes.description: One or more source note lines. Each line is rendered as a small mono paragraph prefixed with 'Source:' or 'Compiled:' as appropriate.

items.description: A source note line, e.g. 'Open Research Onboarding Analytics warehouse (event-level, FY2025); KYC decision logs; cohort funnel reconstructed from 4,206,118 sessions across 18 markets. Figures rounded.'.

meta.title: The State of Digital Onboarding 2026
meta.subtitle: How 4.2 million prospective customers moved from first tap to a funded account this year — the numbers, the funnel, and where the journey still leaks.
meta.author: Research Desk

stats.label: End-to-end completion
stats.note: Applicants who reached a funded account, up from 54% a year ago.

stats.label: Median time to fund
stats.note: From app open to first deposit — nearly half of last year's 9m 12s.

stats.label: Identity-step drop-off
stats.note: The single largest leak in the funnel — still the priority to fix.

bands.heading: Four steps from first tap to funded
bands.subtext: Every onboarding now follows one streamlined path. Median minutes spent at each stage are shown below the step.

items.title: Sign up
items.desc: Email or phone, single screen, no password required up front.

items.title: Verify identity
items.desc: Document scan plus liveness check; auto-decision in seconds.

items.title: Open account
items.desc: Product selected, terms accepted, account number issued instantly.

items.title: Fund & activate
items.desc: First deposit lands; card and app fully active.

bands.heading: Where the 4.2 million go
bands.subtext: Of everyone who opened the app, just over two-thirds reach a funded account. The widest leak is still identity verification.

bands.heading: Not all funded accounts are equal
bands.subtext: Funded customers stratify into a value pyramid — a narrow base of premium relationships sits above a wide foundation of everyday accounts.

bands.heading: Two years of compounding gains
bands.subtext: The same onboarding flow, measured in 2024 and again in 2026, after the streamlined identity step shipped.

items.caption: Completion when onboarding ran on the legacy seven-screen flow.
Completion on the four-step flow with instant identity decisions.

bands.subtext: Faster, simpler onboarding now brings in $1.4 billion in new deposits a year. Close the 22% identity-step leak and another ~850,000 accounts reach funding — the clearest growth lever for 2026.

notes: Open Research Onboarding Analytics warehouse (event-level, FY2025); KYC decision logs; cohort funnel reconstructed from 4,206,118 sessions across 18 markets. Figures rounded.
Compiled: Research Desk · 2026-06-22 · figures illustrative / fictional.
